candidates.title: Burnaby Youth Soccer Association (BYSA) recreational programs
candidates.description: BYSA runs seasonal recreational and competitive soccer leagues and skills programs for school-age children across Burnaby. A natural fit for a child who loves soccer and sports.
candidates.coverageNote: Well-established community soccer association in Burnaby; specific season schedules and registration windows should be confirmed at bysa.ca.

candidates.title: Burnaby Parks & Recreation multi-sport drop-in (community centre gyms)
candidates.description: Burnaby's network of community centres (e.g. Bonsor, Cameron, Edmonds, Eileen Dailly) regularly offer drop-in gym time and multi-sport sessions for children. Great for kids who want to try different sports in an unstructured setting.
candidates.coverageNote: Multi-sport drop-ins are a standard offering at Burnaby rec centres; exact days and age bands vary by location and season — check the Burnaby Parks & Recreation ActiveBurnaby registration portal.

candidates.title: Burnaby FC / local soccer club structured skills training
candidates.description: Several soccer clubs based in Burnaby (including Burnaby FC) offer weekly skills and training sessions for children, bridging recreational play and competitive development. Good for a child ready to go beyond league play.
candidates.coverageNote: Club-level programs are active in Burnaby; rosters, tryout dates, and fees should be verified directly with the club.

candidates.title: Burnaby Lake Regional Park — open field kick-about & nature trail
candidates.description: Burnaby Lake Regional Park has open grassy areas well-suited for informal soccer practice alongside scenic trails. Combines the sports interest with outdoor activity at no cost.
candidates.coverageNote: Burnaby Lake Regional Park is a confirmed public park operated by Metro Vancouver Parks; no booking required for open green space use.

candidates.title: Burnaby Parks & Recreation after-school sports programs
candidates.description: The City of Burnaby runs seasonal after-school programs at community centres that include soccer, floor hockey, basketball, and other team sports for school-age children. Structured coaching in a fun, social environment.
candidates.coverageNote: After-school sport programs are a documented part of Burnaby's recreation offerings; current session availability should be confirmed on the ActiveBurnaby portal.

candidates.title: Swangard Stadium public watch events & community soccer days
candidates.description: Swangard Stadium in Central Park, Burnaby is a local soccer landmark that occasionally hosts community soccer days and youth events around Pacific FC or other matches — a motivating experience for a young soccer fan.
candidates.coverageNote: Swangard Stadium is a confirmed venue in Burnaby; community youth day events are plausible but not individually verified — check pacificfc.ca or the City of Burnaby events calendar for upcoming dates.

candidates.title: Burnaby Central Park — open field & paved courts for sports play
candidates.description: Central Park in Burnaby offers large open grassy areas, basketball courts, and paved paths ideal for pick-up soccer and other sports. A free, accessible outdoor option for active kids any day of the week.
candidates.coverageNote: Central Park is a well-known, confirmed City of Burnaby park with open recreational space; no booking needed.
